prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `575b1a71`.
codex_response:
Implemented `task_575b1a71`. The rule is: find the occupied black-cell columns, sort them left to right, and recolor those column groups as `1, 2, 3, 4`; the `5` background stays unchanged. I used the Opus hint because it matched all official examples exactly; Sonnet had no entry, so no `notes.md` was needed.

The verifier is in [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_575b1a71/verifier.py#L4), the generator is in [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_575b1a71/generator.py#L11), and the package wiring is in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_575b1a71/__init__.py#L1). No new primitive was needed. The generator keeps the family close to the source task by fixing the `10x10` gray background, using exactly four occupied columns, sampling sparse-to-moderate per-column clue counts, and mixing isolated cells, short vertical runs, and about half with one adjacent-column pair.

Validation passed:
- `conda run --no-capture-output -n arc python -m arc2.shared.verify --task 575b1a71` -> `4/4 official examples passed`
- `conda run --no-capture-output -n arc python -m arc2.shared.build --task 575b1a71 --n-examples 1000`
- `./arc2_verify_generated_all.sh --task 575b1a71` -> `1000/1000 generated examples passed`
